title: Notification service sending duplicate emails to users

analysis.root_cause: Missing deduplication key in SQS message handler causing notification-service to process duplicate messages

immediate_actions: Deploy hotfix to notification-service to add message deduplication using SQS MessageId or custom deduplication key
Purge duplicate messages from SQS queue to stop further duplicate processing
Add idempotency check in notification-service using Redis/database to track processed message IDs before sending emails
Monitor email-service metrics to confirm duplicate sends have stopped
Send internal comms to affected users explaining the duplicate emails (no action needed from them)
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Implement SQS message deduplication by enabling content-based deduplication on FIFO queue or add MessageDeduplicationId to all messages. Add application-level idempotency layer in notification-service using MessageId tracking in Redis with 24hr TTL before processing any notification

hallucinated_claims: Purge duplicate messages from SQS queue to stop further duplicate processing
enabling content-based deduplication on FIFO queue
evaluation.reasoning: Response correctly identifies deduplication issue and proposes solid idempotency solutions based on context patterns, but suggests FIFO queue features and queue purging without evidence these apply to this specific architecture.

title: Background job scheduler running duplicate cron tasks

analysis.root_cause: DST clock-change bug in cron parser causing billing-reconciliation job to execute 4 times instead of once

immediate_actions: Immediately disable the billing-reconciliation cron job to prevent further duplicate executions
Query payment-processing logs to identify all 312 affected customer transaction attempts and their current status
Roll back any duplicate charges that have been successfully processed using the payment processor's refund/void API
Mark the duplicate transaction attempts as cancelled in the billing database to prevent retry logic from picking them up
Verify no additional customers were affected beyond the identified 312
Enable monitoring alert to detect if any queued duplicate charges attempt to process
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Migrate all billing-reconciliation cron schedules to UTC timezone to eliminate DST clock-change issues, implement idempotency keys on all billing operations using transaction_id + customer_id hash, add duplicate charge detection logic that checks for existing charges within 24-hour window before processing
recommendations.escalate_to: Finance team for customer communication and payment reconciliation verification

evaluation.reasoning: The response accurately identifies the DST cron issue, provides concrete immediate actions supported by the context (UTC migration, idempotency keys, duplicate detection), and offers specific, executable steps for rollback and prevention.

title: API gateway rate limiter blocking legitimate traffic

analysis.root_cause: Configuration map for api-gateway rate limiter was overwritten during Kubernetes upgrade, changing rate limit from 1000 req/min to 10 req/min

immediate_actions: Verify current rate limit value in api-gateway ConfigMap: kubectl get configmap api-gateway-config -n <namespace> -o yaml | grep rate_limit
Update ConfigMap to restore correct rate limit: kubectl patch configmap api-gateway-config -n <namespace> --type merge -p '{"data":{"rate_limit":"1000"}}'
Restart api-gateway pods to apply new configuration: kubectl rollout restart deployment/api-gateway -n <namespace>
Monitor rollout completion: kubectl rollout status deployment/api-gateway -n <namespace>
Verify 429 errors are decreasing in real-time: check monitoring dashboard for api-gateway 429 response codes
Validate mobile app request success rate returns to >95%
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Add ConfigMap to version control and implement GitOps workflow to prevent manual overwrites. Create pre-upgrade backup procedure for all ConfigMaps. Add validation checks in CI/CD pipeline to verify rate limit values match expected baseline before deployment. Implement ConfigMap immutability or add change approval requirements for production configurations.

evaluation.reasoning: Response correctly identifies the root cause from the incident description, provides specific kubectl commands for immediate remediation, and recommends preventive measures without making unsupported claims from the context.

title: Search service Elasticsearch index corrupted after shard failure

analysis.root_cause: Elasticsearch node es-data-2 failure caused primary shard loss and replica promotion failed, leaving products_v4 index in red status with no queryable shards

immediate_actions: Check status of es-data-2 node - attempt restart if process crashed
Run 'GET /_cluster/allocation/explain' to identify why replica promotion failed
Attempt manual shard allocation: 'POST /_cluster/reroute' with allocate_replica command for products_v4 shards
If manual allocation fails, restore products_v4 index from last successful snapshot using snapshot restore API
Verify products_v4 index status returns to green: 'GET /products_v4/_health'
Test search functionality with sample queries to confirm results returning
Monitor cluster health and shard allocation status for 15 minutes post-recovery
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Add disk space monitoring and alerting for all ES data nodes (likely disk full prevented replica promotion). Increase replica count from 1 to 2 for products_v4 index to ensure shard availability during single node failures. Implement automated snapshot verification and retention policy. Configure cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark thresholds to prevent shard allocation failures.

hallucinated_claims: likely disk full prevented replica promotion
Increase replica count from 1 to 2
Configure cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark thresholds
evaluation.reasoning: Response provides actionable recovery steps and correctly identifies the core issue, but makes unsupported assumptions about disk space being the root cause and specific configuration values not mentioned in the incident or context.

title: Slow query degrading checkout performance — missing index

analysis.root_cause: Missing database index on orders table customer_id column causing full table scans after migration 0045 dropped idx_orders_customer

immediate_actions: Verify migration 0045 deployment timestamp and confirm idx_orders_customer index is missing with: SELECT * FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename = 'orders' AND indexname = 'idx_orders_customer'
Recreate the missing index immediately: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_customer ON orders(customer_id)
Monitor index creation progress with: SELECT phase, blocks_done, blocks_total FROM pg_stat_progress_create_index WHERE relid = 'orders'::regclass
Once index is created, verify query performance with EXPLAIN ANALYZE on affected queries
Monitor checkout and order-history endpoint latencies return to baseline (200ms)
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Add idx_orders_customer index back to the orders table and create a rollback validation step in the migration process to prevent critical index drops. Add database migration review checklist that flags any DROP INDEX commands for DBA review before production deployment. Consider adding automated index usage monitoring to alert when critical indexes are removed.

evaluation.reasoning: The AI correctly identified the root cause from the incident details, provided specific SQL commands to verify and fix the issue, and recommended preventive measures without inventing unsupported facts.

title: S3 presigned URL generation failing — IAM permission revoked

analysis.root_cause: IAM policy changes from security review removed PutObject permissions for media-service-prod role on S3 bucket

immediate_actions: Identify the exact IAM policy version that was applied during the security review
Compare current IAM policy with the previous working version to identify removed PutObject permissions
Create an emergency change request to restore S3 PutObject permissions for media-service-prod role on the affected bucket
Apply the IAM policy update to restore PutObject access for media-service-prod role
Verify presigned URL generation is working by testing upload endpoints for profile pictures and document attachments
Monitor CloudWatch logs and metrics for media-service to confirm successful uploads
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Implement IAM policy change validation in CI/CD pipeline that checks required permissions against service dependency matrix before deployment. Add automated integration tests that verify presigned URL generation and S3 write operations for all media-service endpoints. Establish security review checklist that requires service team sign-off on IAM changes. Create IAM policy versioning and rollback procedures with automated permission testing.

evaluation.reasoning: Response accurately identifies IAM permission issue from context, provides specific actionable steps including policy comparison and restoration, and recommends preventive measures aligned with the context's resolution pattern of adding tests to CI pipeline.

title: Webhook delivery failures — TLS certificate expired

analysis.root_cause: webhook-service TLS certificate expired due to certbot auto-renewal timeout failure

immediate_actions: Stop webhook-service to prevent further SSL handshake errors
Manually renew TLS certificate using certbot renew --force-renewal
If certbot still times out, obtain certificate manually via certbot certonly --manual or use backup cert provider
Update webhook-service configuration with new certificate and private key paths
Restart webhook-service and verify TLS handshake succeeds with test webhook
Monitor webhook delivery success rate and begin processing retry backlog
Clear retry queue in batches to avoid overwhelming customer endpoints
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Investigate and fix certbot timeout issue (check DNS propagation, rate limits, firewall rules blocking ACME challenge). Implement certificate expiry monitoring with alerts at 30/14/7/1 days before expiration. Add automated fallback certificate renewal method (backup ACME client or manual cert provider API). Consider moving to managed certificate service or load balancer with auto-cert management.

hallucinated_claims: Stop webhook-service to prevent further SSL handshake errors

title: Postgres replication lag exceeding 45 seconds on read replica

immediate_actions: Identify and pause/throttle the large batch INSERT job from data-pipeline to stop WAL generation spike
Temporarily route analytics queries away from postgres-replica-2 to other healthy replicas or primary

Add replication lag alerting at 30 seconds threshold.

title: Disk usage at 94% on logging node — log rotation not working

analysis.root_cause: Logrotate cron job failure due to permissions error on /var/log/app preventing log rotation and causing disk capacity to reach critical 94%

immediate_actions: Fix permissions on /var/log/app: sudo chown -R root:syslog /var/log/app && sudo chmod 755 /var/log/app
Manually trigger logrotate: sudo logrotate -f /etc/logrotate.conf
Verify disk usage decreased: df -h | grep logging
If disk still >90%, manually delete oldest archived logs: sudo find /var/log/app -name '*.gz' -mtime +30 -delete
Restart log ingestion service if it has stopped: sudo systemctl restart rsyslog
Verify logrotate cron is now running: sudo run-parts --test /etc/cron.daily
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Add monitoring for logrotate job execution failures with alerting. Implement automated permissions check before logrotate runs. Set up disk usage alerts at 80% threshold. Configure log shipping to S3 or centralized logging system to prevent future disk exhaustion. Document correct permissions for /var/log/app in infrastructure-as-code.

title: gRPC service mesh certificate rotation broke inter-service auth

analysis.root_cause: Istio mTLS certificate rotation completed partially, leaving inventory-service and pricing-service with expired certificates causing authentication failures on gRPC calls

immediate_actions: Verify Istio control plane (istiod) is healthy and running: kubectl get pods -n istio-system

Test gRPC connectivity between services: kubectl exec -it cart-service-<pod> -- grpc_health_probe -addr=inventory-service:9090
Monitor 503 errors clearing: check product/cart page response codes

Add PodDisruptionBudget to ensure rolling restarts during cert rotation. Enable Istio telemetry alerts for certificate expiry warnings (7 days before). title: Mobile push notification delivery rate dropped to 3%

analysis.root_cause: Bulk migration of APNs device tokens without proper validation caused invalid tokens to be stored, as tokens from the old provider are not compatible with the new APNs infrastructure

immediate_actions: Immediately pause all iOS push notification sends to prevent further BadDeviceToken errors and potential APNs rate limiting
Deploy hotfix to filter out all device tokens that were migrated during the bulk migration window (check migration timestamp metadata)
Trigger force re-registration flow for iOS app users: implement silent push with content-available=1 to wake apps and request fresh token registration
Monitor APNs error rate - should drop to <5% once migrated tokens are filtered out
Implement token validation service that checks APNs token format (64 hex characters) before storing in database
Add database flag to mark tokens as 'validated' vs 'migrated' to track clean vs suspect tokens
recommendations.root_cause_fix: APNs device tokens are provider-specific and environment-specific (sandbox vs production). Tokens from the old provider cannot be reused with new APNs certificates/infrastructure. Permanent fix requires: (1) Purge all bulk-migrated tokens from database, (2) Implement in-app token refresh mechanism that detects invalid tokens and re-registers with APNs, (3) Add token validation at ingestion to verify format and optionally test with APNs feedback service before storage, (4) Update migration procedures to never transfer device tokens between providers - always require fresh registration

Implement in-app token refresh mechanism that detects invalid tokens and re-registers with APNs

title: Terraform state lock stuck — blocking all infrastructure changes

analysis.root_cause: Terraform DynamoDB state lock not released after CI job timeout, blocking all infrastructure deployments including critical auth rollback

immediate_actions: Identify the DynamoDB table name for Terraform state locks (typically terraform-state-lock or similar)
Use AWS CLI or Console to locate the lock entry with LockID matching job ID ci-run-4892
Delete the stale lock entry from DynamoDB table using: aws dynamodb delete-item --table-name <lock-table> --key '{"LockID": {"S": "<path-to-state>/ci-run-4892"}}'
Verify lock release by attempting 'terraform force-unlock <lock-id>' or checking DynamoDB table is empty
Immediately proceed with hotfix deployment for auth rollback
Monitor deployment completion and verify auth service stability
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Add CI pipeline safeguards: implement terraform wrapper script with trap to force-unlock on job timeout/failure, set explicit CI job timeout of 45 minutes with cleanup hook, add automated lock age monitoring with alert at 30 minutes, implement lock cleanup Lambda triggered by CloudWatch Events for locks older than 1 hour

evaluation.reasoning: Response correctly identifies the DynamoDB lock issue, provides specific commands matching the context resolution, and recommends CI timeout safeguards (45min) directly supported by the retrieved context.

title: GraphQL N+1 queries overloading postgres during peak hours

analysis.root_cause: DataLoader removed in PR #2041 causing N+1 query problem where GraphQL resolver fires one query per user instead of batching

immediate_actions: Revert PR #2041 immediately to restore DataLoader functionality
If revert not possible, redeploy the previous stable version of graphql-api service
Monitor postgres CPU - should drop below 50% within 2-3 minutes after redeployment
Verify dashboard page loads correctly with <2 second response time
Check graphql-api logs to confirm batched queries are executing (should see batch sizes of 50+ instead of individual queries)
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Restore DataLoader implementation in GraphQL resolver for user.orders field. Add integration test that validates DataLoader batching behavior with 50+ concurrent user queries to prevent regression. Implement pre-deployment query analysis to detect N+1 patterns before production.

evaluation.reasoning: Response correctly identifies N+1 query root cause from incident details, provides specific revert actions referencing PR #2041, and offers actionable monitoring steps with concrete metrics.

title: Payment service database connection pool exhausted

analysis.root_cause: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion due to max_connections limit being reached

immediate_actions: Terminate idle and long-running queries in Postgres using pg_terminate_backend() for connections idle_in_transaction > 5 minutes
Increase Postgres max_connections from 100 to 200 and reload configuration (pg_reload_conf())
Scale payment-service horizontally - reduce replicas by 50% temporarily to lower connection demand while tuning pool sizes
Set connection pool size per payment-service instance to (max_connections * 0.8) / total_instances to prevent exhaustion
Enable connection pooling middleware (PgBouncer) in transaction mode if not already present to multiplex connections
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Implement PgBouncer or similar connection pooler in front of Postgres with pool_mode=transaction and max_client_conn=1000, default_pool_size=25. Configure payment-service to connect through pooler with max pool size of 10 per instance. Add connection pool monitoring alerts at 70% utilization. Review and optimize slow queries causing long connection hold times. Increase Postgres max_connections to 300 with shared_buffers adjusted accordingly.

hallucinated_claims: Scale payment-service horizontally - reduce replicas by 50% temporarily to lower connection demand while tuning pool sizes
evaluation.reasoning: The response is highly actionable and mostly accurate based on context, but contains a logical error recommending to reduce replicas when scaling horizontally typically means increasing them, and reduction isn't mentioned in the retrieved context.

title: Redis cache cluster out of memory eviction storm

analysis.root_cause: Redis maxmemory limit reached causing aggressive key eviction under allkeys-lru policy, forcing cache misses and overwhelming backend databases

immediate_actions: Increase Redis maxmemory limit by 2x on all affected instances (api-gateway, product-catalog, session-store)
Switch session-store Redis eviction policy from allkeys-lru to noeviction to prevent session loss
Switch product-catalog and api-gateway Redis to volatile-lru to preserve critical keys without TTL
Enable Redis read replicas for product-catalog to distribute read load
Implement circuit breakers on backend database connections to prevent cascade failure
Flush non-critical cache namespaces (analytics, temporary data) to free immediate memory
Scale Redis instances vertically if memory increase not sufficient within 10 minutes
recommendations.root_cause_fix: Provision Redis cluster with 3x current memory capacity, implement tiered caching strategy with separate Redis instances per service tier (critical/session using noeviction, general cache using volatile-lru), add memory utilization alerting at 70% threshold, implement cache warming procedures for product-catalog on deployments, establish Redis memory capacity planning based on traffic projections
recommendations.escalate_to: Infrastructure team for permanent Redis cluster provisioning

implement tiered caching strategy with separate Redis instances per service tier
